events.title: AHRQ terminates dozens of patient care research grants: Report
Federal agency for healthcare quality cancels $109 million in grants

ahrq-grant-terminations.summary: On July 20, 2026, reports emerged that AHRQ had terminated dozens of patient care research grants, followed by confirmation on July 22 that the federal healthcare quality agency canceled $109 million in grants. The situation reflects a significant and recent funding withdrawal affecting healthcare quality research. For our research office, this signals potential exposure for any active or pending AHRQ-funded projects and warrants immediate review of affected awards.
ahrq-grant-terminations.title: AHRQ grant terminations

events.title: Pentagon expands blacklist to include China's top civilian universities

pentagon-university-blacklist.summary: On July 23, 2026, the Pentagon expanded its blacklist of foreign universities to include China's top civilian institutions. The action currently broadens restrictions to encompass leading civilian, not just military-linked, universities. This may complicate or restrict research collaborations and partnerships with the affected Chinese institutions.
pentagon-university-blacklist.title: Pentagon blacklist of foreign universities

events.title: White House unveils science funding overhaul
White House Science Report Calls For Reducing University Research Funding

science-funding-overhaul.summary: On July 22, 2026, the White House unveiled a science funding overhaul, followed the next day by a science report calling for reduced university research funding. The initiative currently signals a policy direction toward cutting federal support for university-based research. This could mean significant funding pressures ahead for university research operations.
science-funding-overhaul.title: White House science funding overhaul
